Package io.forja.components.typography.fxLink

Class FxLink

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.control.Control
javafx.scene.control.Labeled
javafx.scene.control.ButtonBase
javafx.scene.control.Hyperlink
io.forja.components.typography.fxLink.FxLink
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget, javafx.scene.control.Skinnable
public class FxLink extends javafx.scene.control.Hyperlink
A styled hyperlink component built on top of Hyperlink.

FxLink extends the standard JavaFX Hyperlink - all native JavaFX properties, bindings, and event APIs remain fully accessible. Forja adds a visual variant system, an automatic external-link icon, and a fluent builder API.

The preferred way to construct an FxLink is via the builder:

     
      FxLink docs = FxLink.builder()
          .text("Open docs")
          .variant(LinkVariant.EXTERNAL)
          .onAction(e -> openDocs())
          .build();

Constructor and direct instantiation are also supported:

     
      FxLink link = new FxLink("Read more", LinkVariant.DEFAULT);

The LinkVariant.EXTERNAL variant automatically appends a trailing external-link icon (via FxIcon). Setting the variant away from EXTERNAL clears the managed icon but leaves any caller-supplied graphic untouched.

See Also:
  • LinkVariant
  • FxLink.Builder

      public FxLink()

    • FxLink

      Creates an FxLink with the given text and variant.
      Parameters:
      text - the text displayed by the link
      variant - the visual variant - see LinkVariant
